name: Safety Risk Framing appraisal for Tad Mood Wellbeing Quality Life bundle
summary: Accepted. The mood/well-being claim incorporates the published safety baseline and does not present tadalafil as an off-label mood or well-being intervention. It carries ETLAS-2 adverse-event imbalance, notes limited adverse-event extraction from ED/BPH abstracts, and keeps current label contraindication and interaction boundaries near the interpretation.

findings.claim_or_issue: The claim support map includes current CIALIS label safety findings from the published safety baseline.
findings.why_it_matters: Benefit-oriented off-label interpretations must keep contraindications, hypotension interactions, and adverse-event warnings visible.

findings.claim_or_issue: The ED/BPH abstracts do not provide full adverse-event tables for this first mood baseline.
findings.why_it_matters: Mood/well-being synthesis should not imply safety clearance in healthy off-label users from abstract-level tolerability language.
findings.recommended_action: Keep safety caveats in public copy and rely on the dedicated safety baseline for contraindication and interaction boundaries.
